name: Mauka Warriors Luau
tagline: Newer Kapolei luau with a 4.9 rating and nearly 5,000 reviews — intimate, warrior-themed, and heavy on audience interaction.
hours: Check-in ~4:30pm, show runs until ~8pm. Days vary seasonally
bestTime: Book a week ahead, especially for weekend shows. The bus pickup option from Waikiki is convenient
parking: Free parking on-site at 91-1780 Midway St, Kapolei
talkStory: The newcomer challenging Toa Luau for best luau on Oahu. Mauka Warriors leans into the warrior culture of Polynesia — expect a more high-energy, athletic performance with Samoan fire knife and Maori haka alongside traditional hula. The intimate setting (smaller than the resort mega-luaus) means you're close to the action. The imu ceremony (digging up the underground-roasted pig) is interactive, not just a photo op. Food is solid traditional Hawaiian buffet. They offer hotel pickup from Waikiki which is a huge plus. The 4.9 rating across nearly 5,000 reviews puts it in rarefied air.
mustOrder: kalua pig from the imu ceremony
try the poi — they make it approachable for first-timers
premium seating is worth the upgrade for front-row fire knife
